summary: Full test pass. Backend (pytest: 5/5 passed in ~17s) and Frontend (Playwright multi-flow) both verified. Health endpoints, PDF-to-mindmap happy path + invalid/empty error paths, landing CTAs, studio sidebar, new/open/delete maps, node add/rename/delete (with root protected), zoom in/out/fit, search modal, export download, PDF upload UI end-to-end, and localStorage persistence across reload all working. Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations returned a well-structured 21-node mind map for the generated Photosynthesis test PDF in ~15s.

minor.issue: Observed transient 502 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.024309, Max budget: 0.001' from LiteLLM under EMERGENT_LLM_KEY during rapid consecutive calls. Endpoint recovered and returned 200 on retry; backend correctly surfaces the error as 502. This is an LLM key/budget configuration concern, not a code bug.

issues: Toast 'Exported as JSON' visually overlaps the subtle 'DOUBLE-CLICK TO RENAME' hint line at bottom-center — minor, non-blocking

action_items: OPTIONAL: If the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget cap (0.001) is intentionally low, user-facing PDF upload should display a clearer error message (e.g., 'AI temporarily unavailable, please retry') rather than a generic toast — currently the frontend keeps the dialog open on 502.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is referenced at lines 171/195/201 inside the handler but only defined at line 230 at module scope. This works at runtime (handler executes after import-time initialization) but is a readability smell — move logging setup above the handlers.
server.py MindMapNode.model_rebuild() is called, but the self-referential typing works; no action required.
parse_json_from_response uses re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?', '') which only strips a leading fence; LLM responses rarely include fences but the fallback regex for '{...}' is a good safety net.
mindmap_from_pdf does not enforce a max page count — a 25MB PDF with many pages could still yield long AI latency. Current 60k char text cap mitigates this sufficiently.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all flows green: landing, studio load, new map, add child, rename, delete node, root-no-delete, zoom fit, open/delete map modal, search modal, export download, PDF upload end-to-end, persistence across reload)

test_credentials: N/A — app is local-first, no auth
seed_data_creation: Generated a 2-page Photosynthesis PDF on the fly via reportlab inside the backend pytest fixture (also reused for the UI E2E test at /tmp/photosynthesis.pdf).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_mindmapper_api.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_mindmapper_api.py -v`. They generate a fresh PDF via reportlab for every run (no external fixtures). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is very tight (0.001 USD) and may transiently 502 on rapid successive PDF calls; if you hit it, wait a few seconds and retry. The 'root' node id in locally-created maps is NOT 'root' — it's a randomly generated 'm_*' id (newId() in /app/frontend/src/lib/storage.js). AI-generated maps use 'root' as the root id. When writing Playwright tests that target the root, detect the id from the DOM instead of hard-coding.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Only observed concern: transient 502 from /api/mindmap/from-pdf due to LiteLLM per-request budget cap (0.001 USD) being exceeded. Repro: POST multiple PDFs back-to-back within a short window. Mitigation: backend already returns 502 with a clear message; could add client-side retry-with-backoff or a nicer user-facing toast on failure.
